title: couralia toxophora
intro: Couralia toxophora, also known as the 'Stinking Cassia', is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant has been reported to have several health benefits, including the reduction of fever, relief from respiratory issues, and treatment of skin conditions. Couralia toxophora can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically used in traditional medicine practices.
